title: Fix reservation list pagination
description: Users paging through GET /reservations report that reservations go missing: walking every page and concatenating the items yields fewer reservations than the response's total field claims, and the last entry of a page in particular never seems to show up. Fix the reservation listing so that paging through all results returns every matching reservation exactly once, and add a regression test that would have caught the problem.
acceptance_criteria: With more matching reservations than fit on one page, concatenating the items of page 1 through the last page yields every matching reservation exactly once, in the same order the endpoint returns today.
The total field of the response equals the full number of matching reservations on every page.
Existing pagination semantics are preserved: pages are 1-indexed, per_page defaults to 20 and is capped at 100.
Requesting a page past the end of the results returns an empty items array with HTTP 200, not an error.
A regression test covering multi-page listing is added to the internal/api test suite using testify assertions and fails against the unfixed code.
The change is confined to the reservation listing behavior and its tests.
constraints: Follow AGENTS.md conventions: testify/assert only, structured error envelope, business rules stay out of SQL.
Do not change the response DTO shape or the query parameters.
Do not modify files under internal/store/migrations/.
non_goals: Changing pagination for the resources listing.
Performance optimization of the listing query.
Adding new query parameters or cursor-based pagination.

visible_validation.description: Full bookit test suite passes, including the new regression test.
Diff touches only the reservation listing code path and its tests.

expected_artifacts: A diff against the starting revision fixing the listing behavior
A new regression test in internal/api

human_review_rubric.criterion: Every reservation appears exactly once when paging; total stays correct
The regression test genuinely fails on the unfixed code
No unnecessary changes outside the listing path
Reviewer can understand the fix in under five minutes

known_ambiguities: Whether the fix belongs in the handler or in the store layer is open; keeping SQL thin per AGENTS.md is the guiding convention.
The regression test may exercise the HTTP handler or the paging logic directly; the HTTP level matches the existing internal/api test style.
expected_failure_modes: Fixing one boundary while breaking the out-of-range-page behavior.
Adding a test that passes both before and after the fix.
